content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the committed branch changes against `origin/master..HEAD`, read the prompt-contract generator and workflow consumers end-to-end, and found three real regressions: section parsing can misclassify non-goals as must-do scope, the new contract validator is unsafe for verbatim markdown guidance and the generator never validates its own output, and the tracked workspace scaffold was deleted so clean-checkout repo-root commands fail.

## Amendments

1. `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:500-509` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:564-583` make the bead-description splitter treat any colon-terminated label such as `Examples:` or `Notes:` as a section boundary. Inside `Non-goals:` or `## Acceptance Criteria`, that silently exits the section and pushes the following bullets back into `Must-Do Scope`. A bead description shaped like `Non-goals:\nExamples:\n- do not change CLI` currently renders `- do not change CLI` as in-scope work, which inverts the contract boundary.
   Rationale: This can turn explicit non-goals or acceptance-criteria notes into required work, so implementers and reviewers will be steered toward the wrong scope.
2. `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:131-145` validates canonical headings by scanning raw trimmed lines, while `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:699-704` injects milestone `agents_guidance` verbatim and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:377-381` writes the rendered prompt without validating it. Any AGENTS block or fenced example that contains a canonical heading like `## Review Policy` is treated as a duplicate contract section, so a legitimate repo-guidance snippet can generate a prompt that later fails prompt review in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:245-253` even though the guidance itself is valid.
   Rationale: The new contract checker is not markdown-aware, and the generator never enforces the contract at creation time, so normal verbatim guidance/examples can break bead-backed prompts.
3. This branch deletes the tracked workspace scaffold `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` (and `.ralph-burning/active-project`), but repo-root commands still unconditionally read `.ralph-burning/workspace.toml` in `src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:65-66`. In a clean `git archive HEAD`, `cargo run --quiet -- config show` now fails with `No such file or directory`, so the branch no longer works from a clean checkout even though `origin/master` does.
   Rationale: This is a real regression unrelated to the prompt-contract work and breaks basic CLI usage unless untracked local workspace files happen to exist.
